- Models/TicketData.cs - input data model
- Models/TicketPrediction.cs - prediction output model
- Services/TicketClassifierService.cs - ML.NET training + prediction


- Controllers/TicketController.cs - API endpoints
- Update Program.cs to register services and add the ML.NET package to the project file

data.content:
Here's a summary of what was implemented:

### Files created/modified

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `TicketClassifier.csproj` | Added `Microsoft.ML 4.0.2` |
| `Models/TicketData.cs` | ML input model with `[LoadColumn]` attributes |
| `Models/TicketPrediction.cs` | ML output model with predicted label + scores |
| `Models/TicketRequest.cs` | API request DTO |
| `Models/PredictionResponse.cs` | API response with category + confidence scores |
| `Models/EvaluationMetrics.cs` | Metrics DTO with per-category precision/recall |
| `Services/TicketClassifierService.cs` | Trains SDCA MaximumEntropy classifier on featurized text + priority |
| `Controllers/TicketsController.cs` | `POST /api/tickets/classify` and `GET /api/tickets/metrics` |
| `Program.cs` | Wires up the singleton classifier service |

### Endpoints

- **`POST /api/tickets/classify`** — Accepts `{subject, description, priority}`, returns predicted category with per-class confidence scores
- **`GET /api/tickets/metrics`** — Returns macro/micro accuracy, log-loss, and per-category precision/recall

### ML Pipeline

Text featurization (Subject + Description) → concatenate with Priority → MinMax normalization → SDCA MaximumEntropy multiclass trainer. Input validation rejects empty text and out-of-range priority values.
